title: epipactis latifolia subsp. violacea
intro: Epipactis latifolia subsp. violacea, also known as the violet helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of iridoid glycosides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The violet helleborine has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
